solution final
solution the core issue in the erroneous code lies in failing to handle duplicate values by not separating them into odd and even indices effectively
solution a correct approach involves sorting the array and then placing the first half into odd positions and the second half into even positions in reverse order
solution this specific strategy ensures that equal elements are never adjacent because they are distributed to alternating indices
solution for the follow up question regarding O(n) time complexity without sorting, one can use a virtual indexing trick combined with a quickselect algorithm to find the median
solution this median partitioning allows us to place elements smaller than the median in one set and larger elements in another without a full sort
solution regarding negative integers, the logic remains identical because the comparison operators and relative ordering work the same for negative values as they do for positive ones
solution the final implementation must check if adjacent elements are equal after the initial pass and swap if necessary to guarantee the wiggle condition
